summary_en: Emimen to acquire all shares of Caremix K.K. to make it a wholly-owned subsidiary. Caremix operates senior-home placement services and nursing care outsourcing across Japan, with ~30 coordinators. Combined group will have ~180 coordinators, expanding service coverage and strengthening competitive position in elderly care and social-issue resolution. Purchase price not disclosed. Transaction resolved by Emimen board on 15 May 2026.
title_en: Notice regarding acquisition of shares in Caremix Co., Ltd. (making it a wholly-owned subsidiary)
translation_en_brief: Emimen Co., Ltd. announced on 15 May 2026 that its board of directors has resolved to acquire all shares of Caremix K.K. and make it a wholly-owned subsidiary. Caremix, headquartered in Machida, Tokyo, operates a senior-home placement and matching service centred on approximately 30 coordinators (primarily in the greater Tokyo area) and provides nursing-care outsourcing services nationwide, including event medical support, accompaniment services for medical procedures or relocation, bespoke home-visiting nursing, medical-care support for school-age children, GPS-tracking for elderly persons with dementia, and nursing-focused staffing services. It also runs a children's charitable book-donation bookstore. Emimen's core business is senior-home placement consultation and referral services for elderly individuals and their families facing complex care needs (business/young/elder-elder caregiving, cognitive decline, high medical dependency). The two companies share a management philosophy of solving social issues through business operations. The integration is expected to expand the combined group's coordinator base to approximately 180 professionals, enhance service quality, broaden geographic coverage, and strengthen competitive advantage in elderly-care solutions. Purchase price and transaction timing details were not disclosed in the announcement.
